summary: The team held a two-week retrospective on the Comply v2 launch, reviewing adoption metrics, support volume, and customer feedback. Adoption reached approximately 38-39% of eligible accounts (847 out of ~2,200 tenants), significantly exceeding the internal 25% benchmark. HIPAA reports tracked higher than expected, and a minor ISO 27001 formatting issue is being addressed in an upcoming patch. The team also identified promising cross-sell signals, with customers discovering gaps in their IAM configuration through SOC 2 reports and subsequently inquiring about Aegis Identity.
actionItems: Megan Lawson: Prepare a one-pager for the exec review covering adoption numbers, HIPAA findings, and cross-sell signals
Tom Bradley: Deploy the ISO 27001 control numbering formatting fix in the next patch by end of next week
Diana Reeves: Pull customer testimonial quotes from recent QBRs, including the healthcare company audit prep quote
Megan Lawson: Initiate internal conversation about scoping and resourcing GDPR framework addition

keyMoments.text: Megan shares that 847 unique tenants generated at least one report in two weeks, beating the internal 25% adoption benchmark with ~38-39% adoption
keyMoments.speaker: Megan Lawson

keyMoments.text: Brian notes ongoing ISO 27001 formatting issue where control numbering doesn't match customer expectations
keyMoments.speaker: Brian Cho

keyMoments.text: Diana reports at least four active upsell conversations driven by customers discovering IAM gaps through SOC 2 compliance reports
keyMoments.speaker: Diana Reeves

keyMoments.text: Jordan raises GDPR as the next framework to add, with Diana confirming strong demand from the European segment since Q3 last year
keyMoments.speaker: Jordan Whitfield
